source.title: Cafe Dostoyevsky Cocktail Window Execute - 5-Stack Ranked Replay Breakdown (Ying/Sens/Gridlock/Amaru/Nomad)
source.notes: Auto-generated captions (ASR). This video walks through exactly one attack plan (a 5-stack execute through the cocktail window on the top-floor site) using a match replay from the presenter's stream, rather than a full map guide across all sites — so this file only covers that one site/plan. ASR renders 'Sens' as 'sense'/'sc', 'Amaru' as 'amaru'/'a Maru', and 'Ying' as 'ying' throughout — these are all real Attack operators and match context (light sensors/roll sensor wall = Sens; grapple/vault = Amaru; Candela = Ying) so they're used as-is. Defender operators named in the replay (Valkyrie, Mute, Kapkan, Warden, Vigil) are opponents, not attack picks, and are only referenced for context, not listed as recommendations in this Attack file.

gadgetPlacements.note: Repel up to the cocktail window, smoke off the double door, then fire multiple Candelas into the cocktail area along the main sightline plus at least one Candela out into the hallway beyond. Repels in afterward and plants in the corner by the bomb.
Place an Air Jab Launcher trap on the pillar door.

gadgetPlacements.location: White stairs window, AT THE BOTTOM of the window frame
gadgetPlacements.note: Second Air Jab trap, placed low to prevent a run-out through this window. Critical detail: the Air Jab only triggers when the target lands, not mid-fall — placing it at the top of the window means the target is already out of range by the time they hit the ground, so it must be at the base of the window to actually go off.
After placing both Air Jab traps, repels up to the cocktail window and holds a tight angle through cocktail from there once everyone else is in position.

Throws (pre-primes, unarmed, then activates) a roll sensor down through the skylight to cut the cocktail sightline from roughly mid-room to the back, blinding anyone holding an angle down cocktail (e.g. a defending Valkyrie).

gadgetPlacements.location: Cocktail window (after the sensor curtain is up)
gadgetPlacements.note: After throwing the roll sensor, either stays on the skylight or repels to the cocktail window to hold and control it — whoever holds this window can deny both the plant and the defuse.
Opens the roof hatches early to sell a normal/expected push — every round typically starts with hatches opening, so this doesn't tip the actual plan.

gadgetPlacements.location: Skylight, into freezer doors / bar storage doors / down onto cocktail
gadgetPlacements.note: Throws Trax Traps through the skylight toward the freezer doors and bar storage doors, and down onto cocktail, to generate noise and can eventually drop the skylight itself.
Additional Trax Traps thrown through the doors in this area purely to add noise/annoyance and mask the real push.

gadgetPlacements.location: Heaven Balcony or White Stairs window
gadgetPlacements.note: Repels in once the plant is starting (after Ying's Candelas and the smoke are down) — normally comes through Heaven Balcony/window, but in the replay came through White Stairs window instead and it worked; pushes down cocktail or holds from Heaven balcony to deny the plant/defuse.

operatorRecommendations.note: Leads the execute: smoke + Candela spam into cocktail, repels in, and plants in the corner by the bomb (or optionally on top of the cocktail desk near B1/B2 as a safer alternative plant spot noted later in the video).
Roll sensor curtain through the skylight blinds any defender watching down the cocktail sightline (including from cameras); can also hold cocktail window afterward.
Opens hatches to sell a normal push, then spams Trax Traps through the skylight and nearby doors for noise and denial while staying on the roof/skylight.
Repels through Heaven Balcony (or White Stairs window as a viable alternate) once the plant starts, to help deny defenders pushing the plant.
Air Jab traps on pillar door and the base of the white stairs window stop defenders running out or repelling to contest; then holds the cocktail window angle.

generalTips.note: Works with as few as 2-3 players, though 5 is ideal — scale the roles down by dropping the lowest-priority ones (Gridlock's noise/distraction role) if short-handed.

This same window-execute concept can be adapted to other sites/maps with a similar window setup — the video specifically mentions CCTV on Club House and Kitchen-equivalent windows on other maps as comparable targets.

Once the plant is down with two attackers still on repel outside (cocktail window + heaven/white stairs), the round becomes very hard for defenders to win — described as '99 times out of 10' successful by the presenter, though he caveats this is tested at Champion-level lobbies, not guaranteed at lower ranks.

Alternative plant spot: standing on top of the cocktail desk keeps the planter hidden from every angle a defender could hold from, at the cost of being exposed if a defender walks through the first sensor curtain near that spot.

Defender counters observed/discussed: a defender playing Pulse below, a defender C4-ing into cocktail blind if they suspect someone's inside, or a defender holding the corridor door and trying to peek/deny from there — none of these fully stopped the execute in the replay because the sensor curtain plus smoke removed all information.

In the replay round, the defending team's positioning was a mismatch for this plan (Warden roaming near bakery on the far side of the map instead of anchoring near the plant with his shield/C4, which the presenter calls the defenders' biggest mistake) — don't assume every defense will be this exploitable, but the plan is designed to punish exactly this kind of split.
